Bots are Good Listeners

How do all of these bots know what to do?

Each of the buttons is programmed to shout a specific command.

And each of the bots is programmed to listen for those commands.

You already know how to make bots listen for @onClick. In this tutorial, you'll learn how to make them listen for whatever you want!

Listening

Each of the buttons at the bottom are programmed to shout the following commands:

  • moveUp
  • moveDown
  • moveLeft
  • moveRight

The bot above is already programmed to listen to "moveUp" in its @moveUp tag

You just need to program the other three directions

Hint

The bot already knows how to "moveUp," so you can program the other 3 directions in similar ways.

Instead of adding to tags.homeY, you might need to add to (or subtract from) tags.homeX

Solution

Each of the other three tags should be programmed like this:

@moveDown

tags.homeY = tags.homeY - 1

@moveRight

tags.homeX = tags.homeX + 1

@moveLeft

tags.homeX = tags.homeX - 1

If your bot is not reacting to any of the directions, make sure the tag is listening with the orange '@' symbol in the top right while you're editing its tag.

Shouting

This bot already know how to respond to:

  • jump
  • bounce
  • spin
  • slide
  • changeColor

You need to program the button to shout a command when you click on it.

Hint

"shout" is a function, so you will need () after it to make it execute.

Remember that since you just want to shout a word or command, you will need to put your command in quotation marks (")

Solution

Any of the following will make the bot do something:

shout("jump")

shout("bounce")

shout("spin")

shout("slide")

shout("changeColor")

If nothing is happening, make sure the button's onClick is still listening with the orange @ symbol in the top right.

Whispering

Whispering is a lot like shouting, except you can specify which bot(s) can hear the whisper.

See if you can make the button whisper to Bob instead of Alice.

These bots both know how to respond to all of these commands:

  • jump
  • bounce
  • spin
  • slide
  • changeColor
Hint

In order to whisper to a bot, your code needs to know what bot you want to communicate with.

You can do this in a few ways, but here we will get the bot with the getBot() function.

The getBot() function needs two things: a tag and a value the label should be. Then, it will find a bot with a tag that matches.

Solution

This will find the bot with the #label "Bob" and whisper to it:

var thatBot = getBot("label", "Alice") whisper(thatBot, "slide")

If nothing is happening, make sure the button's onClick is still listening with the orange @ symbol in the top right.
